ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general laksa entry; no dedicated vegetarian laksa node exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_description: Noodle soup dish — composite mixed dish with vegetables and tofu in spiced coconut broth
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific vegetarian laksa code. A0FKP (mixed composite dish) is the closest defensible anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for vegetarian laksa as a specific food concept.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard hawker-centre serving of vegetarian laksa is approximately 400 g, comprising a bowl of coconut-milk broth with rice noodles, tofu puffs, and vegetables.

nutrient_source.title: Vegetarian Laksa — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Vegetarian Laksa (cr_id F06010005). All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this entry at confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values — rice noodle soup analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for vegetarian laksa. GI of 66 is estimated based on the analogous rice noodle soup entry (GI ~65–68) from the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed Atkinson et al. 2008 tables. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (66 × 43.1) / 100 ≈ 28.5 for a 400 g serving (43.1 g carbohydrate). The input record cited GI 66 / GL 7.1 per 100 g which is consistent with (66 × 10.73) / 100 = 7.08.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 351 mg per 100 g; a 400 g bowl delivers approximately 1,404 mg sodium, representing 61% of the 2,300 mg daily limit.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to half a bowl, avoid consuming the broth, and request reduced laksa paste and salt when ordering; potassium intake from this dish is low (~110 mg/100 g) and provides insufficient offset to the high sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium GI of 66 with moderate carbohydrate content (10.73 g/100 g); a 400 g serving delivers approximately 43 g carbohydrate and a glycemic load of ~28.5.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should limit the noodle portion and increase tofu and vegetable components to reduce the glycemic load; the coconut milk fat content moderates gastric emptying and glucose absorption, but the overall carbohydrate load per full bowl remains significant and should be accounted for in the daily carbohydrate budget.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat estimated at approximately 3.8 g per 100 g from coconut milk, contributing roughly 15.2 g saturated fat per 400 g bowl, which approaches or exceeds the recommended daily limit of 20 g for cardiovascular risk management.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size to half a bowl and request reduced coconut milk or a lighter broth to lower saturated fat intake; the high sodium content (351 mg/100 g) also poses an independent cardiovascular risk and should be managed by avoiding the broth.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Chilli (nightshade) is a primary ingredient in laksa paste; solanine-containing nightshades may aggravate inflammation in sensitive individuals.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should monitor personal tolerance to nightshade ingredients in laksa paste (chilli, pepper) and consider requesting a reduced-paste preparation; turmeric in the paste provides some curcumin anti-inflammatory benefit.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Vegetarian Laksa is a Singaporean Peranakan-style spicy coconut milk noodle soup that replaces the traditional seafood or chicken with tofu, tau pok (fried tofu puffs), and mixed vegetables. The dish is built on a rich, aromatic broth made from coconut milk, laksa paste (containing chilli, lemongrass, galangal, turmeric, and a vegetarian substitute for shrimp paste), and vegetable stock. It is served over rice vermicelli or thick rice noodles and garnished with bean sprouts and fresh herbs. The flavour profile is bold, spicy, and creamy with a characteristic coconut-forward richness balanced by the heat of chilli and the earthiness of turmeric. Per 100 g, it provides 101 kcal, 2.14 g protein, 5.49 g fat (predominantly saturated from coconut milk, estimated ~3.8 g/100 g), and 10.73 g carbohydrate. Sodium is elevated at 351 mg/100 g, making it a concern for hypertension management. The glycemic index is approximately 66 (medium), driven by the rice noodle base. It is suitable for vegetarians and is commonly found at hawker centres, school canteens, and food courts across Singapore. Clinically, it is moderate concern for type 2 diabetes due to its medium GI and carbohydrate load, high concern for hypertension due to sodium, and moderate concern for cardiovascular disease due to saturated fat from coconut milk.

Can I eat laksa with high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F06010005) at confidence 0.95. GI estimated from the closest analogous rice noodle soup entry in the University of Sydney GI Database, consistent with the input record's cited GI of 66. Saturated fat estimated from coconut milk composition data consistent with HPB and USDA reference values. All mandatory multilingual names provided.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions (monounsaturated, polyunsaturated, trans), micronutrient panel (calcium, iron, cholesterol, vitamins A/B1/B2/B3/B6/B12/C/D/E, folate), and moisture data for this composite vegetarian laksa preparation are not individually reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry F06010005 as of 2026; these fields were confirmed unavailable upon review of the HPB Foodo database and USDA FoodData Central for this specific composite dish. Saturated fat (3.8 g/100 g) is estimated from coconut milk composition consistent with HPB and USDA FoodData Central reference data for coconut milk and is not a direct HPB-reported value. Potassium (110 mg/100 g) is an estimate referenced from the input record's clinical notes and is not a directly reported HPB value; it is flagged for verification. GI of 66 is an analogous estimate based on rice noodle soup entries in the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct measurement for vegetarian laksa exists in the approved GI source. Glycemic load per serving recalculated for a 400 g standard bowl: (66 × 43.1 g carbohydrate) / 100 ≈ 28.5. The input record cited GL 7.1 per 100 g which is consistent with (66 × 10.73) / 100 = 7.08. is_vegan set to false as traditional laksa paste may contain shrimp paste; vegetarian versions may vary — verify with vendor. FoodOn match is approximate (general laksa node); no dedicated vegetarian laksa FoodOn URI confirmed. FoodEx2 code A0FKP used as closest defensible composite dish anchor.
